digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design reference and skill integration
propositions: Create a canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals with the scale-to-the-bug principle.
Each consuming skill directory ships a byte-identical real-file copy of test-design.md referenced by its own SKILL.md, with no symlinks or machine-specific paths.
A gating unit test test_skill_shared_sync.py asserts each copy is byte-identical to the canonical file and self-verifies its detection logic via a perturbed tmp_path copy.

Integrate the shared standard into rebar-debug (Step 4 retitled to RED-oracle design, LLM-surface paragraph and taxonomy Phase 2 defer to quantified evals, Step 5 defect-seeded mutation and contract-card handoff), rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b, and rebar-brainstorm's ticket-recording section.
Document the canonical-file plus byte-identical-copies convention, sync one-liner, and drift test in README.md.
All prompt edits address the LLM skill user, lead with positive guidance, and avoid elaborated negative examples per the pink-elephant rule.
